author: René Descartes
text: I think, therefore I am.

author: Friedrich Nietzsche
text: It is my ambition to say in ten sentences what everyone else says in a whole book – what everyone else does not say in a whole book.

author: Friedrich Nietzsche
text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.

author: Karl Marx
text: The philosophers have only interpreted the world, in various ways; the point, however, is to change it.

author: Plato
text: The heaviest penalty for declining to rule is to be ruled by someone inferior to yourself.

author: Plato
text: The unexamined life is not worth living.

author: Aristotle
text: All men by nature desire to know.

author: Friedrich Nietzsche
text: He who cannot command himself should obey.

author: George Orwell
text: Who controls the past controls the future. Who controls the present controls the past.

author: Benjamin Franklin
text: Those who would give up essential Liberty, to purchase a little temporary Safety, deserve neither Liberty nor Safety.

author: Ludwig Wittgenstein
text: The limits of my language mean the limits of my world.

author: Marcus Aurelius
text: Confine yourself to the present.

author: Jean-Paul Sartre
text: Man is condemned to be free.

author: Thomas Hobbes
text: Covenants, without the sword, are but words.

author: George Orwell
text: All animals are equal, but some animals are more equal than others.

author: Friedrich Nietzsche
text: He who fights with monsters should look to it that he himself does not become a monster. And when you gaze long into an abyss the abyss also gazes into you.

author: Epicurus
text: Accustom yourself to believe that death is nothing to us, for good and evil imply awareness, and death is the privation of all awareness; therefore a right understanding that death is nothing to us makes the mortality of life enjoyable, not by adding to life an unlimited time, but by taking away the yearning after immortality.

author: Seneca
text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.

author: Gilles Deleuze
text: A concept is a brick. It can be used to build a courthouse of reason. Or it can be thrown through the window.

author: Plato
text: Is the pious loved by the gods because it is pious, or is it pious because it is loved?

author: Friedrich Nietzsche
text: God is dead! God remains dead! And we have killed him!

author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
text: Man is born free, and everywhere he is in chains.

author: Friedrich Nietzsche
text: How much truth can a spirit endure, how much truth does a spirit dare? That became for me more and more the real measure of value.

author: Franz Kafka
text: A book must be the axe for the frozen sea within us.
source: Letters to Friends, Family, and Editors

author: Thucydides
text: The strong do what they can and the weak suffer what they must.

author: William Shakespeare
text: Cowards die many times before their deaths; the valiant never taste of death but once.
source: Julius Caesar, Act II Scene II

author: Plato
text: Rhetoric is the art of persuasion which produces belief, not knowledge.
